1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: Antenatal care is best defined as:
options: A preventive obstetric program from conception to onset of labor
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Antenatal care is a preventive obstetric program including observations, medical care, investigations and advice from conception to labor onset.

1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: All of the following are objectives of antenatal care EXCEPT:
options: Giving advice for all pregnant patients
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Objectives include identifying/monitoring high-risk pregnancy, early detection of complications, advice, and managing minor problems. Labor management is intrapartum care.

1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: Which of the following best defines this condition?
options: A pathological deviation from normal anatomy or function
An incidental finding with no clinical significance
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.
